title: Apparatus and method for filtering radio frequency signals of transceiver integrated circuits
abstract: Devices and methods are disclosed for generating, filtering, and amplifying signals that are sent and received using SOCs. These improved methods and devices advantageously provide filtering of composite RF signals such that the RF signals can be transmitted with an improved SNR. Such filtered signals can then be transmitted at a higher power. Because filtering is performed at an intermediate frequency, the higher cost of low-noise RF-transmitters and/or RF filtering components can be avoided. Accordingly, less expensive (e.g., noisier) components, such as readily available wireless transceiver SOCs, can be used for generating RF signals, filtering the signals, and then transmitting the filtered signals at higher power. As a result of these devices and methods, inexpensive SOCs may be used at higher powers and over longer ranges than would be normally expected
claims:
Claims CLAIMS We claim: 1. A wireless device comprising: a system on a chip (SOC) having a first output wherein the SOC is configured to generate a composite radio frequency (RF) signal at the first output wherein the composite RF signal comprises a first RF signal having a first frequency and a second RF signal having a second frequency and wherein the first frequency belongs to a first frequency band and the second frequency belongs to a second frequency band, the first frequency band being different from the second frequency band; a local oscillator (LO) configured to generate a LO signal; and a transceiver comprising a down converting mixer, a first band pass filter, and an up converting mixer, wherein a first down converting mixer input of the down converting mixer is connected with the first output and a second down converting mixer input of the down converting mixer is connected with an output of the LO and a down converting mixer output of the down converting mixer is connected with a first band pass filter input of the first band pass filter and wherein a first up converting mixer input of the up converting mixer is connected with a first band pass filter output and a second up converting mixer input of the up converting mixer is connected with the output of the LO. 2. The wireless device of claim 1 further comprising: a first switch disposed between the down converting mixer output and the first band pass filter input such that the first band pass filter input is connected with a first output terminal of the first switch and the down converting mixer output is connected with a common input terminal of the first switch; and 

 a second switch disposed between the first band pass filter output and the second up converting mixer input such that the first band pass filter output is connected with a first input terminal of the second switch and the up converting mixer input is connected with a common output terminal of the second switch. 3. The wireless device of claim 2 further comprising a second band pass filter disposed between the first and second switches such that a second band pass filter input of the second band pass filter is connected with a second output terminal of the first switch and a second band pass filter output of the second band pass filter is connected with a second input terminal of the second switch. 4. The wireless device of claim 3 wherein the down converting mixer is configured to shift the frequency of the first RF signal to a first intermediate frequency and the frequency of the second RF signal to a second intermediate frequency. 5. The wireless device of claim 4 wherein the SOC is further configured to operate the first and second switches to cause one of the first or second band pass filter to filter the second RF signal. 6. The wireless device of claim 5 wherein the up converting mixer is configured to shift the frequency of the first RF signal from the first intermediate frequency to the first frequency. 7. The wireless device of claim 6 further comprising a power amplifier (PA) connected with an up converting mixer output and configured to amplify the first RF signal. 8. The wireless device of claim 7 further comprising an antenna connected with an output of the PA and configured to transmit the amplified first RF signal. 9. A method comprising: generating a composite radio frequency (RF) signal wherein the generated composite RF signal comprises an information RF signal having a first frequency and a noise RF signal having a second frequency; shifting the composite RF signal so that the information RF signal and the noise RF signal are shifted to a respective first and second intermediate frequency; filtering the shifted composite RF signal to remove the shifted noise RF signal; and shifting the composite RF signal so that the shifted information signal is shifted from the first intermediate frequency to the first frequency. 10. The method of claim 9 wherein shifting the composite RF signal comprises mixing the composite RF signal with a local oscillator (LO) signal. 11. The method of claim 10 further comprising programming a local oscillator (LO) to cause the LO to generate the LO signal. 12. The method of claim 9 further comprising, based on a bandwidth of the wireless communication channel, selecting from a set of band pass filters, a band pass filter wherein a pass band of the selected band pass filter is substantially equal to the bandwidth of the wireless communication channel. 13. The method of claim 12 further comprising causing the selected band pass filter to filter the shifted composite RF signal to remove the shifted noise RF signal. 14. The method of claim 9 further comprising amplifying the shifted composite RF signal before filtering the shifted composite RF signal. 15. The method of claim 10 further comprising: generating a phase shifted LO signal that is phase shifted by 180 degrees from the phase of the generated LO signal such that radiated energy from generating the phase shifted LO signal substantially cancels radiated energy from generating the LO signal. 16. A multiple-in-multiple out (MIMO) device comprising: a first transceiver; a second transceiver; a local oscillator (LO) configured to generate an LO signal, wherein energy corresponding to the LO signal is radiated; and a phase shifter coupled to the LO and configured to phase shift the LO signal to generate a phase shifted LO signal, wherein energy corresponding to the phase shifted LO signal is radiated, the phase shifter coupled with the first transceiver to provide the LO signal to the first transceiver and coupled with the second transceiver to provide the phase shifted LO signal to the second transceiver, wherein the radiated energy corresponding to the LO signal cancels the radiated energy corresponding to the phase shifted LO signal. 17. The MIMO device of claim 16 wherein a mixer of the first transceiver is configured to receive the LO signal and mix a composite RF signal generated by an SOC of the MIMO device with the generated LO signal. 18. The MIMO device of claim 16 wherein a mixer of the second transceiver is configured to receive the phase shifted LO signal and mix a composite RF signal generated by an SOC of the MIMO device with the phase shifted LO signal. 19. The MIMO device of claim 16 wherein the phase shifter is configured to phase shift the generated LO signal by 180 degrees. 20. The MIMO device of claim 16 wherein a respective output of the first transceiver and the second transceiver is connected with a MIMO antenna.
